description: A title can change while the person who holds it remains a 'miona', one human being. Gender and social standing take their own descriptions. 'limoe' (creature) reaches more broadly across animate life.
articulatory_notes: Close the lips for voiced 'm', let the sound resonate through the nose, and release into high-front unrounded 'i'. Keep stressed middle-height back rounded 'o' in its own syllable across the hiatus. Touch the tongue tip to the upper teeth for dental 'n', then lower the tongue and unround the lips into open central 'a'.
usage_notes: A numeral may take optional 'himo' (people classifier) before this noun. A name remains a separate designation under 'ne'; personhood alone neither creates nor replaces a name.

pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Keeping 'miona' available beside titles and group labels lets a sentence return to the human participant when a category starts to eclipse them.

semantic_domains.community: a human participant in social and relational life
